title: AI Superpowers for Small Business

modules.title: AI Tools Any Business Can Afford
modules.sub: Discovering budget-friendly AI tools built for small businesses
modules.description: Learners explore a curated landscape of accessible, low-cost AI tools suited to small business needs. They evaluate options across categories such as scheduling, communication, and analytics to identify which tools align with their current operations and budget.

modules.title: Automating Customer Service With AI
modules.sub: Using chatbots and AI to handle customer interactions efficiently
modules.description: Learners set up and configure basic AI-powered customer service tools, including chatbots and automated response systems. They practice designing conversation flows that reflect their brand voice while reducing the manual workload on their team.

modules.title: Smarter Marketing Using AI Insights
modules.sub: Leveraging AI data to sharpen targeting and campaign decisions
modules.description: Learners use AI-driven marketing tools to analyze customer behavior, segment audiences, and generate campaign ideas. They make decisions about where to focus marketing spend based on insights surfaced by AI platforms.

modules.title: Inventory and Operations Made Easier
modules.sub: Applying AI to streamline stock, scheduling, and daily workflows
modules.description: Learners identify operational bottlenecks in their business and explore AI tools designed to automate inventory tracking, demand forecasting, and task scheduling. They map out a practical plan for integrating at least one operational AI tool into their workflow.

modules.title: Writing and Content With AI Assistance
modules.sub: Creating business content faster using AI writing tools
modules.description: Learners experiment with AI writing assistants to draft emails, social media posts, product descriptions, and other business content. They develop a personal editing process to ensure AI-generated content maintains accuracy, tone, and authenticity.

modules.title: Protecting Your Business Data
modules.sub: Understanding data risks when adopting AI tools and platforms
modules.description: Learners examine the data privacy and security considerations that come with using third-party AI tools in a small business context. They review best practices for data handling, vendor permissions, and employee access to reduce exposure and maintain customer trust.

modules.title: Choosing and Evaluating AI Vendors
modules.sub: Asking the right questions before committing to an AI provider
modules.description: Learners build a practical vendor evaluation framework covering cost, scalability, support, and data practices. They apply this framework to compare real AI vendors and make a simulated purchasing recommendation for their business scenario.

modules.title: Building an AI-Ready Business Culture
modules.sub: Preparing your team to adopt and grow with AI tools
modules.description: Learners assess their current team's readiness for AI adoption and identify common resistance points and skill gaps. They draft a lightweight change management plan that introduces AI tools gradually, builds staff confidence, and sets expectations for ongoing learning.

synopsis: Small business owners often assume AI is only for big corporations with big budgets — this course proves otherwise. From chatbots to content creation, learners will discover practical, affordable AI tools and how to integrate them into daily operations.
rationale: A demand signal of 5/10 combined with a corpus similarity of 0.61 against the narrow 'AI-Assisted Budget Forecasting' indicates that small business owners need a broad, practical AI literacy course that spans operations, marketing, and vendor decisions rather than finance alone.
